title: Introduction to C++
description: What C++ is, why it's used, how it works, and why it matters for DSA and interviews.
objectives: Understand what a programming language is and what C++ is
Understand why C++ is used and where
Understand the C++ Code → Compiler → Machine Code flow at a high level
Understand why C++ suits DSA and coding interviews
Read a first C++ program without needing to fully explain it yet

sections.text: 1. What is C++?

C++ is a programming language. A programming language allows us to give instructions to a computer. Think of a computer like a very fast worker — but it does not understand normal English instructions. You cannot simply tell a computer "Add two numbers and show me the answer." Instead, we write instructions using a programming language.
C++ is a general-purpose programming language used to create fast and efficient software.
2. Why do we use C++?
One major reason is performance. C++ programs can be very fast. C++ also gives programmers more control over how a computer uses its memory and resources.
For our learning journey, we are mainly using C++ for: DSA + LeetCode + Coding Interviews.
3. How does C++ work?
Suppose we write this C++ code:

The computer's processor cannot directly understand this C++ code. The processor understands machine instructions. Conceptually, we can imagine them as binary instructions like 10110100, 11001010, 00110101. So our C++ code must be translated.

sections.title: Translator analogy
sections.text: Imagine you speak English. Another person understands only Japanese. You say "Hello". A translator converts your message into Japanese. Similarly: C++ Code → Compiler → Machine Instructions. The compiler acts like a translator.
We have not officially learned the Compiler yet — only the word is introduced here. We will study the Compiler and Compilation Process as a separate topic.
4. Why is C++ good for DSA?

Suppose we want to store many numbers. C++ provides powerful tools for this. Later, you will learn things like vector, stack, queue, map, unordered_map, set, priority_queue. These tools are part of the Standard Template Library, commonly called STL.
Do not worry about these names now. You are not expected to understand or use them yet — each one will be learned separately before being used in DSA problems.
5. When should we use C++?

sections.title: C++ is a strong choice when
points: We need better control over memory
We are preparing for coding interviews

sections.text: For example, imagine we need to process 1,000,000 numbers — efficiency becomes important. Later, you will learn how C++ and DSA help us write efficient solutions.
6. Real-Life Example

sections.title: Chef and recipe
sections.text: A recipe says: 1) Take two eggs, 2) Break the eggs, 3) Mix them, 4) Heat the pan, 5) Cook the eggs. The chef follows the instructions step by step. Programming works similarly — we write instructions (Step 1, Step 2, Step 3, Step 4) and the computer executes them.
Programming is giving step-by-step instructions to a computer.
7. First Look at C++ Syntax

You do not need to understand this code yet. It contains multiple new concepts — #include, iostream, namespace, std, main(), cout, return. We learn a concept before expecting you to use it, so these are covered one by one later. For now, only understand: this is an example of a C++ program.
8. C++ Syntax

Syntax means the rules for writing code. In English, we may write "I am learning C++." Writing "Learning am I C++." does not follow normal English sentence structure. Programming languages also have rules.
Writing something incorrectly may cause an error. For example: cout "Hello"; — C++ may reject this instruction because the syntax is incorrect.
Syntax is the set of rules used to correctly write code in a programming language.

sections.title: Common Beginner Mistakes
items: Trying to memorize code like #include <iostream> / using namespace std; / int main() without understanding it.
Thinking you need to learn all C++ syntax before writing any code — instead, build gradually: Concept → Understand → See Example → Trace Code → Practice → Use in DSA.
Jumping straight into LeetCode before arrays, loops, hashing, STL containers, and complexity analysis are clear — this leads to memorizing solutions instead of learning problem solving.

sections.title: Interview Questions

questions.question: What is C++?
questions.answer: C++ is a general-purpose programming language known for high performance and control over system resources. It is commonly used in system programming, game development, competitive programming, and Data Structures and Algorithms.

questions.question: Why is C++ popular for DSA?
questions.answer: C++ is popular for DSA because it is fast and provides the Standard Template Library, which contains useful data structures and algorithms. (STL itself is studied later.)

questions.question: Is C++ a compiled language?
questions.answer: Yes. C++ source code is translated into machine code using a compiler before the program is executed. The exact compilation process is a later topic.

questions.question: What is syntax?
questions.answer: Syntax is the set of rules that defines how code must be written correctly in a programming language.

questions.question: Why are we learning C++ for coding interviews?
questions.answer: C++ provides good performance and powerful STL containers and algorithms that help us implement DSA solutions efficiently.

sections.thirtySecondAnswer: C++ is a general-purpose, compiled programming language known for its performance and control over system resources. It is widely used in system programming, game development, competitive programming, and DSA. C++ is also popular in coding interviews because its Standard Template Library provides useful containers and algorithms for implementing efficient solutions.

sections.title: Trick to Remember C++
sections.text: C++ = SPEED + CONTROL + DSA — SPEED (fast programs), CONTROL (better control over computer resources), DSA (excellent ecosystem for coding problems).

sections.title: Quick Understanding Check
questions: What is C++ in your own words?
Why is C++ popular for DSA and coding interviews?
What is the role of a compiler?
